commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the lot of the children of Joseph fell from the Jordan by Jericho, unto the water of Jericho on the east, to the wilderness that goes up from Jericho throughout mount Bethel. The double portion is given to him, as Ruben forfeited his birth-right. (Chaldean) (Worthington) Waters; or the celebrated fountain, which renders the territory so fruitful, and which was made sweet, by Eliseus casting salt into it, 4 Kings ii. Wilderness of Bet haven, chap. (Calmet) Which, is not to be referred to wilderness, but to the word lot. (Masius; Menochius).
